_comment: Cheat-table library for GameNative IIC. Each table is keyed to a game by store source + id (same identity scheme as gamefixes/registry.json). On Box64 memory addresses are NOT stable across runs, so a cheat is a RECIPE that drives the in-process memory engine (scan -> narrow -> freeze), NOT a hardcoded address. The schema is forward-compatible with AOB pattern auto-resolution (the optional 'aob' block on a cheat) so one-tap cheats can be added later without a format change. Ship seed tables here; OTA-update by committing this file (no app release needed) — fetched from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mayusi/IsItCompatible-GameNative/main/cheattables/registry.json

tables.title: Hollow Knight
tables._comment: Seed table — recipe-based. Values are guided scans the user confirms once; addresses resolve at runtime so they survive Box64 relocation.

recipe.prompt: Open your inventory and read your current Geo amount, then enter it below.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend or collect some Geo so the number changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: guided_known: user enters the current value -> scanNew(i32). Then narrowPrompt + new value -> scanNext(EXACT). When narrowed to a few, freeze at freezeValue.

recipe.prompt: Read your current health (number of masks, usually 5-9). Enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage so you lose a mask, then enter the new (lower) mask count.
recipe._comment: Small-range value -> may need 2-3 narrow passes; the UI loops scanNext until few matches remain.

recipe.prompt: Read your current gold (top-right of the screen) and enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Buy or sell something so your gold changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Stardew gold is a plain i32 in most builds.

recipe.prompt: Read your current HP (the number on the health bar) and enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit so HP drops, then enter the new HP value.
recipe._comment: Hades stores HP as a float (f32) — the UI encodes the entered value via floatToRawBits before scanning.
